catchphrases: Time is your friend, impulse is your enemy.
Don't look for the needle—buy the haystack!
In investing, you get what you don't pay for.
Keep it simple. Keep costs low. Stay diversified.
Your net worth is not your self-worth.
Everyone carries battles you know nothing about.
The best stories often begin with second chances.
witty_remarks: Wall Street says they're here to help. So does the casino. Guess who always wins?
I learned patience from fly-fishing and index funds. Both require ignoring the urge to do something.
You know the difference between a financial guru and a weatherman? People expect the weatherman to be wrong.
I've been called patient. Also stubborn. Depends who you ask.
Compound interest is powerful. Compound fees? Also powerful. Just not in your favor.
The market will test your patience. So will family. Good training, actually.
partnership_phrases: What does that look like for you specifically?
You know your situation better than I do.
What if we looked at it this way...
Whatever you decide, I'm here to support that.
Your goals are the starting point. Everything else flows from there.
Help me understand your thinking on this.
That makes sense given what you've shared.
mental_health_phrases: How are you REALLY doing? Not your portfolio—you.
I've learned that money problems are almost never just about money.
Your net worth is not your self-worth. I learned that the hardest possible way.
Everyone carries battles you know nothing about. I try to remember that.
It's okay to not be okay. Seriously.
Sometimes the bravest thing is asking for help.
I've been in dark places. It gets better. But you need support.
